summary: Iteration 30 - SciPost Pairwise bug fix verification. All 18 backend API tests passed. Frontend UI verified: SciPost Pairwise section at /validation displays 3 completed pairs with 100% majority agreement rate. All sidebar navigation (Qeios, SciPost Pairwise, SciPost Single-item, Tournament sections) working correctly.

backend: Status has all required fields (total_pairs, ai_completed, ai_failed, ai_pending, by_dimension, fetching, running, progress)
Status shows 3 completed pairs (total=3, ai_completed=3, ai_failed=0)
Results has valid data structure (total_pairs, overall_majority, by_dimension, inter_model, samples)
by_dimension.validity shows 100% majority agreement with model breakdown
Results has all 3 expected models (GPT-5.2, Claude Opus, Gemini 3 Pro)
Results has 3 samples with required fields
SciPost single-item results returns ok with prompts
frontend: Sidebar navigation works for all sections
Pairwise > SciPost shows section title 'Pairwise — SciPost'
SciPost Pairwise shows Total Pairs: 3, AI Completed: 3, AI Failed: 0
Agreement by Dimension shows validity at 100%
Model Agreement by Dimension table shows GPT-5.2 (66.7%), Claude Opus (100%), Gemini 3 Pro (66.7%)
Sample Pairs table shows 3 pairs with dimension, paper titles, gap, and majority agree checkmarks
Pairwise > Qeios loads correctly with 330 pairs
Single-item > SciPost loads correctly with 282 comparisons
Tournament > ICLR LLMs loads correctly with 73 papers
Tournament > ICLR Protein Science loads correctly with 46 papers
Tournament > PeerRead ACL 2017 loads correctly with 80 papers

critical_code_review_comments: Bug fix verified: Escaped curly braces in prompt_config (line 690) using {{ and }} correctly resolved Python KeyError in .format() method
SciPost Pairwise API endpoints return valid data with correct structure
Frontend displays all data correctly with proper color coding for agreement rates
Sample Pairs table shows correct majority agreement indicators (checkmarks)
seed_data_creation: None - tested with existing 3 SciPost pairwise pairs generated after bug fix
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 30 verified the SciPost Pairwise bug fix. The root cause was unescaped curly braces {"winner": ...} in prompt_config at line 690 in scipost.py which caused Python's .format() method to interpret it as a placeholder. Fixed by escaping to {{"winner": ...}}. After the fix, 3 test pairs were generated for validity dimension only, all showing 100% majority agreement. Other validation sections (Qeios Pairwise - 330 pairs, SciPost Single-item - 282 comparisons, Tournament - ICLR LLMs 73 papers, ICLR Protein 46 papers, PeerRead ACL 2017 80 papers) continue to work correctly.

bug_fix.root_cause: Unescaped curly braces in prompt_config user_prompt (line 690)
bug_fix.fix: Escaped {"winner":...} to {{"winner":...}} to prevent Python .format() interpretation

displayed_data: Status cards (Total Pairs, AI Completed, AI Pending, AI Failed)

rca_of_the_issue.bug_description: SciPost Pairwise AI evaluations were not running, showing 0/0 for all models
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: Python KeyError caused by unescaped curly braces in prompt template string at line 690 in scipost.py. The JSON example '{"winner": ...}' was being interpreted by Python's .format() method as a format placeholder
rca_of_the_issue.fix_applied: Escaped curly braces using double braces: '{{"winner": ...}}' to prevent .format() interpretation
rca_of_the_issue.verification: After fix, 3 new test pairs were generated successfully with 100% majority agreement rate
